provenance_defaults.source: self-authored (general clinical pharmacology knowledge; corroborate against AMH/TGA at review). NOT derived from LITFL notes.

records.subject: dopamine antagonists
records.rationale: dopamine-receptor blockade worsens parkinsonian motor function

records.subject: metoclopramide
records.rationale: dopamine antagonist — worsens parkinsonism and causes extrapyramidal effects (use domperidone if antiemetic needed)

records.subject: typical (first-generation) antipsychotics
records.rationale: dopamine-receptor blockade may worsen/mask tardive dyskinesia; risk of an irreversible movement disorder

records.subject: typical (first-generation) antipsychotics
records.condition: Parkinson's disease or Lewy body dementia
records.rationale: dopamine blockade worsens parkinsonism; severe neuroleptic sensitivity in Lewy body dementia

records.subject: NSAIDs
records.condition: active peptic ulcer or gastrointestinal bleeding
records.rationale: COX inhibition impairs mucosal protection — worsens bleeding/ulceration

records.subject: NSAIDs
records.rationale: sodium and fluid retention worsen decompensation

records.subject: propranolol
records.condition: asthma or severe reversible airways disease

records.subject: sumatriptan
records.condition: ischaemic heart disease or uncontrolled hypertension
records.rationale: coronary vasoconstriction — risk of myocardial infarction/arrhythmia

records.subject: aminoglycosides
records.rationale: neuromuscular blockade worsens weakness (respiratory failure risk)

records.subject: suxamethonium
records.condition: hyperkalaemia, recent major burns or spinal cord injury

records.subject: suxamethonium
records.condition: personal or family history of malignant hyperthermia

records.subject: metformin
records.condition: severe renal impairment (eGFR <30) or acute metabolic acidosis
